diff_summary: Epic-26 merge adds availableLoyaltyToRedeem + markPendingClawback (parentId/pointsDelta/pending_clawback schema) to packages/wallet/src/loyalty-redeem.ts, the availableToRedeem/sumPendingClawback pure helpers to contracts, migration 0085 (pending_clawback column), and unit/integration tests. The actual redeem path (redeemPoints, called by POST /parents/me/loyalty/redeem) is left on the pre-existing P2-E05 walletId/direction/points schema and is NOT wired to the new pending-clawback guard.
ac_coverage: AC1 VIOLATED in the real redeem path (redeemPoints gates on raw P2 walletId balance, never subtracts pending_clawback); the new availableLoyaltyToRedeem proves AC1 only in isolation. AC2 implemented & tested (markPendingClawback provisions a zero-delta row; clawbackForRefund offsets it). AC3 NOT implemented (no UI/contract field exposes available-to-redeem; the platform loyalty page and LoyaltyRedemptionQuote still surface the raw balance).

findings.title: Redeem path does NOT respect pending settlement — availableLoyaltyToRedeem is never called by redeemPoints (AC1 violated, dual-schema gap confirmed)
findings.detail: MONEY-CRITICAL. The only redemption entrypoint is redeemPoints (loyalty-redeem.ts:48), invoked by POST /parents/me/loyalty/redeem (apps/api/src/routes/parents/loyalty.ts:123). Its sufficiency gate is `if (input.points > balanceBefore) throw InsufficientPointsError`, where balanceBefore = getLoyaltyBalance(tx, input.walletId) (loyalty-redeem.ts:84-86). getLoyaltyBalance sums the P2-E05 columns (direction/points) keyed on walletId (loyalty.ts:129-137) and never reads pending_clawback. The new P3-E04 function availableLoyaltyToRedeem (loyalty-redeem.ts:147-161) — which DOES subtract pending — is exported (index.ts:86) and unit-tested but is called by NO production code (grep: only its own def, the wallet barrel, and tests). So a parent CAN redeem points that are about to be clawed back: the exact failure the story exists to prevent. WORSE — the dual-schema gap flagged by the prior P2-E05-S03 review is real and unbridged: schema/loyalty.ts:27-29 documents that P2 rows leave parentId NULL and P3 rows leave walletId NULL. availableLoyaltyToRedeem queries SUM(pointsDelta)/SUM(pending_clawback) WHERE parentId = $parent (loyalty-redeem.ts:151-157); P2 earn rows (which constitute the redeemable balance) have parentId = NULL and are not selected, while markPendingClawback writes rows with parentId set and walletId NULL. The redeemable balance (P2/walletId) and the pending hold (P3/parentId) live in two disjoint partitions of one table, joined by nothing — even if availableLoyaltyToRedeem were wired into redeemPoints it would see a balance of 0 and the parent's real points separately, so the math cannot work as-is. The story file (26-4...md:20,55) explicitly claims 'redeemPoints refuses any redemption exceeding [available-to-redeem]' and 'redeemPoints throws when the requested points exceed available-to-redeem' — both FALSE on the merged working tree. Decision-needed (not patch) because the correct fix requires a product/architecture decision on how the two ledger engines reconcile: either (a) backfill/derive parentId on P2 rows (or walletId on P3 rows) and have redeemPoints compute availableLoyaltyToRedeem over a unified key inside the same locked transaction, or (b) collapse to a single schema. A naive one-line wiring would not fix the parentId/walletId partition.
findings.fix: Wire the gate into the actual redeem path AND bridge the key gap (human decision required). Inside redeemPoints, after locking the wallet row, resolve the parentId for input.walletId (wallets.userId) and compute available = availableToRedeem(P2 balance for walletId, Σ pending_clawback for that parentId) within the SAME transaction, then gate on `input.points > available`. This requires deciding the canonical owner key so the P2 balance and the P3 pending are summed over the same partition — without that reconciliation the SUM(pending_clawback) WHERE parentId query returns 0 for P2-owned wallets and the guard is a no-op. Add an integration test that earns via the P2 path, marks a pending clawback, and asserts redeemPoints rejects redeeming into the held points.

findings.title: AC3 not implemented — no UI or contract field exposes available-to-redeem; redeem surface still shows raw balance
findings.detail: AC3 requires 'UI shows available-to-redeem, not raw balance, on the redeem screen.' The redemption quote contract LoyaltyRedemptionQuote (contracts/src/index.ts:4707-4713) carries only availablePoints (documented at :4708-4709 as 'the balance') and maxDiscountCents — no pendingClawback / availableToRedeem field. The GET /parents/me/loyalty handler (apps/api/src/routes/parents/loyalty.ts:81-98) builds the quote from getLoyaltyTotals(...).balance (raw P2 balance) and never calls availableLoyaltyToRedeem. The parent loyalty page (apps/platform/app/(app)/loyalty/page.tsx:38-42) renders account.balance and account.quote.maxDiscountCents directly. Net: the redeem screen shows the raw balance, exactly what AC3 forbids. The story (26-4...md:22) hand-waves this as 'the parent-facing P2-E05 redemption UI/engine is not present on this branch' — but on the merged working tree it IS present (same loyalty page + redeemPoints in the same module), so the scoping excuse no longer holds. Decision-needed because surfacing available-to-redeem depends on resolving the same parentId/walletId reconciliation as the AC1 finding (the API can't compute a correct available value to put in the quote until the engines are unified).
findings.fix: After the AC1 reconciliation lands: add pendingClawback + availableToRedeem to LoyaltyRedemptionQuote, populate them in GET /parents/me/loyalty from availableLoyaltyToRedeem (computed over the unified owner key), and update the platform loyalty/redeem screen to display available-to-redeem (with a note when balance > available because a settlement is pending). Add a test asserting the quote reflects a pending hold.

findings.title: markPendingClawback / availableLoyaltyToRedeem are not transaction-locked against a concurrent redeem (TOCTOU)
findings.detail: Even once the gate is wired, availableLoyaltyToRedeem (loyalty-redeem.ts:147-161) reads pending via an unlocked SELECT, and markPendingClawback (loyalty-redeem.ts:178-212) inserts a pending row without taking the wallet/parent row lock that redeemPoints uses (loyalty-redeem.ts:60-64). A redeem that reads available-to-redeem before a concurrent markPendingClawback commits could still let points through that a refund is about to hold — the available value is stale between the SELECT and the redeem INSERT unless both serialise on the same lock. redeemPoints already locks the wallet row FIRST precisely to serialise redeems; the pending path must take the same lock (or a parent-scoped advisory lock) for the AC1 guarantee to hold under concurrency. Deferred: this is only exploitable after the AC1 wiring exists, and markPendingClawback is described as a rare admin-initiated workflow (AC2), so it is lower-likelihood than the structural gap above — but it must be addressed in the same fix.
findings.fix: When AC1 is wired, ensure availableLoyaltyToRedeem is read inside redeemPoints' existing FOR UPDATE transaction on a lock shared with markPendingClawback (lock the parent/wallet owner row in both paths), so a redeem and a pending-hold cannot interleave on the same balance.
